content:
Hi, I noticed I might have typed too much that might be putting off people from giving advice so I wanted to just rephrase my questions:

Based on what you see, should I keep pulling characters or start pulling wheels for Standard pulls?
What would you recommend for the Premiums pulls? E3 Kathigu-Ra or Moonless Guide pulls?
Is Caecus worth replacing anyone in the current line up?
1 Week Account Pulling Advice?

Pulling on moonless guide is a good choice
The selection you picked is a very good one
Just don't use up all your funds since Arachne (next new limited character) is coming soon
Mouchette will rerun but might take a while since she's fairly recent
The collab with mist sequence is unique

Mist sequence is the gacha game where Mouchette came from
And it already ended its service (EOS) 
That's why they were able to get Mouchette as a limited character permanently added to the game
So they can rerun her anytime

For advice on Standard banners, you can continue pulling on Realm Study since it will help you make more teams. If you do make that choice, I recommend Caro or Ultra. If you're lucky, you could get older limited characters but see it as bonus and not a goal.
For Ultra, getting Jenkins and Winkle will be your target
idk if you saw my guide already: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?usp=sharing

content: i think you should pull for blade of the titan at some point, even if your main dps is vortice, because you are going to need 5 teams at endgame
and one of them is 100% guaranteed to use it
however it might make more sense to pull E3 memory spiral first
I would pull on realms research because u still lack many standard characters
Caecus is ok, not bad not amazing, u can clear stuff with him, but he will be dead weight in any team that has vortice on it simply because vortice kills too fast for him to do anything
he has his moments

Hey there! Currently only on chapter 2 and need some advice for team building in the future. Though I am a 'New' player, I actually began my journey some time ago but dropped the game, but I'm still only level 16. Hearing the news of a certain other game has recently freed up my time, so I was wondering if there were any teams that Wanda really works with and teams I may want to make as well. 

Currently, I use Wanda, Tawil, Nautilus and Jenkins as my main team.

hi and welcome <:Pandia_love:1459078512303411261> 

I have a guide you can read here

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?usp=sharing

i have some bad news, wanda is a very high investment character, she falls off in endgame unless u hyperinvest, and even then is only good in specific stages
so that is fair warning before you spend any more resources on wanda
she can carry up to midgame but in late game the enemies just hit too hard
Gotcha, so even though I lucked out and got 3 copies of her, it's not gonna help in the long run if I seek to clear all the content, huh.
yeah, she's a one trick pony basically
if her gimmick of reducing str and making the enemies attack a lot is useful, then she's useful
otherwise theres just better units to put in the team
that said, whaling can solve some of wanda's problems lol
like if you have a super stacked high level wanda then her STR down is better against more stages

I don't trust my luck further than what I already somehow managed lmao.

Is Tawil at least a good investment overall? I like her command card copy a lot.
tawil used to be super op, the opinion on tawil here has turned somewhat
people will tell you that shes a bad investment compared to other characters, not necessarily because shes weak, but because she needs more investment to be competitive nowadays
OE tawil is a strong carry
but thats 8 copies of tawil
i do think that E0 tawil is quite weak and best used as a support
at E2 tawil is a decent carry/support engine, not able to dps some harder stages, but able to pull a team together
and she no diffs most low level content
Yeah, anything above 3 copies is not coming across my gaze anytime soon.
anyway its fine
wanda is cool, and if you like her, u will still find ways to play her
its not that shes totally trash, she just falls off a little bit in endgame content, which is still pretty far away from your perspective
u can get her to level 60 no problem
for an early game wanda team i would recommend wanda caecus and 2 flex
heres one i did earlier
the last 2 slots are not terribly important, you can put whoever u have
Gotcha, so I should put some investment into Caecus as basically the 'ride or die' until I get more characters under my umbrella for now.
u dont actually have to pull him, he is fine at e1
just needs to rouse and then he will outheal all of early game
especially if you have his ssr wheel
What about Goliath? I honestly started the game for him. Is he a good choice to have around for investment?

For late game, if u want to play wanda, the strongest comp uses OE daffodil because she's the character that can make the most temporary counter in one turn

So you make a pile of counter with daffodil, use wanda exalt, and oneshot enemies
goliath is mainly used as a support for his exalt, because he can convert tentacle dmg into STR
so he's exponentially multiplying team buffs
his damage is ok at low levels, its all burst damage that doesnt scale well with enemy hp, so he is at most used to clean up normal enemies at endgame
not good for bossing
Gotcha.
caecus goliath used to be the speedrun meta for low levels lol
wanda caecus goliath sanga is probably fine and can get you through all of the story up to like chapter 8
by then u will have pulled more characters and u can see what else u want to build

Alright, then I'll do that and read over your guide.

Thanks a bunch.
enjoy the game <:wish:1356084702468899117>

Ah I see, I will probably hold off until the last day of the Kathigu-Ra banner before I decide. Hopefully Arachne comes out by then. 

I did take your Standard summons advice since I do lack a lot of standard characters. Thanks for the help!

Hi, I have been taking a look through the Guide you had posted mainly using it as a reference on what each characters do and if they fit for teams and I wanted to say thank you for making it! 

It has been extremely helpful for new players like me to get started and understand what to do

I agree with what you are saying related to the Wheels. 

I was mainly wondering in terms of value right now (in terms of pushing further/faster in story) should I bother with Blade of the Titan right now since Vortice doesn't make use of it that well. I already did summons in Realm Study so I will keep that in mind when I think I should start pulling for Wheels

Yeah, Vortice just kills things in 1-3 turns most of the times. While Caecus could stall out the fight and let more Pursuits proc, I didn't feel like I needed it compared to just blocking more damage or having a guaranteed revive with Celeste
Thanks for the advice!
if you dont need it right now then you dont have to pull it <:bow:1356053037159944473>

Really having second thoughts about having dore as my main dps. Now that I'm starting arc 2, I'm thinking about investing in a different character to be the main dps. I have 6 gnostic advance materials I think. Who should I spend them on?
vortice is good, kath is good, xu is good
u already have e3 kath so no issue there
pollux is good too
for reference i was beating arc 2 chapter 1 with this team when i started out
I haven't seen tinct used in any teams in such a long time <:dead:1356082627509092372>
me going back and doing hard mode achievements, tinct still mvp <:shy:1356053151349997829>
